markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Parse specs, WPs, MTs, taskboards, build order, traceability registries, and validation receipts as source records. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: GovernanceArtifactIngestion leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: GovernanceArtifactIngestion is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 4a21588b; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: Bounded + deterministic: max_depth=2, max_spans=200, max_span_chars=4000. Span cap overflow is COUNTED as skipped_nodes -> 'partial' receipt (never silent loss); content truncation is FLAGGED on the anchor (truncated:true), never silent. RFC 6901 pointer escaping (~0/~1) correct.

JSONL: malformed lines skipped + counted (partial); empty artifact or all-malformed -> typed PARSE_ERROR. JSON depth recursion is bounded by max_depth (2) so a deeply-nested adversarial artifact cannot blow the stack.

Governance JSON spans pass redact_spans (secret_scan=true) and the whole-file preflight runs over the raw JSON text, so a credential committed into an MT/WP/receipt JSON value is redacted/blocked. NOTE: governance artifacts are exactly the files most likely to legitimately contain receipt ids that LOOK token-shaped; the entropy gate + placeholder filter reduce false positives but a high-entropy id could be over-redacted (availability, not leak).
evasion_probes: object root -> /key and one nested level /key/sub, /key/index CONFIRMED.
pointer special chars a/b, c~d escaped to /a~1b, /c~0d CONFIRMED.
span cap respected, skipped_nodes counted, long node truncation flagged CONFIRMED.
JSONL line numbers recorded, malformed line counted not fatal CONFIRMED.
broken JSON ({broken) -> typed PARSE_ERROR + repair entry CONFIRMED (pipeline mt089).
bare scalar document -> single whole-document span CONFIRMED.
governance_sub_kind classification (mt_contract/wp_contract/taskboard/...) recorded in provenance CONFIRMED.
evidence_checked: engine.rs:1054-1090 GovernanceArtifact branch (json vs jsonl, lossy->partial)

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); only LOW non-blocking notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.

findings.summary: MT-089 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION but declares MT-081 as a dependency, and MT-081 is still CLAIMED rather than validator-completed.
findings.required_remediation: Do not resubmit MT-089 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

evidence_basis: Authoritative MT JSON contract and current product tree inspection.
Dependency failures use deterministic packet status/dependency reads only; WIP MT work products were not inspected.
remediation_summary: Do not resubmit MT-089 until MT-081 is READY_FOR_VALIDATION and passes WP Validator review, or update the dependency graph through the packet authority if the dependency is no longer real.

remediation_v2.fail_verdict: FAIL_V1 BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time)
remediation_v2.remediation: Dependency resolved: MT-081 is now implemented+hardened+verified (commit 5876feaf) and READY_FOR_VALIDATION. This MT own implementation passed adversarial review; the only V1 blocker was the MT-081 dependency. Resubmitting for V2 re-validation.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-089 requires GovernanceArtifactIngestion: parse JSON/JSONL governance artifacts (WP/MT contracts, receipts, registries) into structural spans with restartable, hash-based, secret-aware, PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed evidence and failure receipts. The code implements exactly this — a bounded deterministic JSON/JSONL parser emitting RFC 6901 JSON-pointer spans, wired into the ingestion engine with real PostgreSQL persistence, EventLedger receipts, governance sub-kind provenance, secret-scan preflight, and repair-queue enqueue on typed parse failure — and is proven by a genuine managed-PostgreSQL pipeline test.
findings: governance.rs:139-243 (wtc-kernel-009/src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_ingestion/governance.rs): parse_governance_json / parse_governance_jsonl emit bounded structural spans with RFC 6901 pointers; token escaping ~->~0, /->~1 at line 67-69; truncation FLAGGED on anchor (render_node 71-79); span cap counts skipped_nodes (emit_node 90-93); JSONL malformed lines counted not fatal, all-malformed/empty -> typed ParseError (227-238). Real implementation, no scaffolding.

Wired into engine: engine.rs:31 imports the parsers; engine.rs:1087-1123 GovernanceArtifact branch dispatches json vs jsonl, maps lossy->Partial + ParseError class, err->Failed; engine.rs:365-368 records governance_sub_kind in provenance via detect_governance_sub_kind (kinds.rs:335). Not dead code.

Secret-scan preflight (engine.rs:1033-1056) runs over the whole raw text for any needs_text kind including GovernanceArtifact; HIGH-severity findings Block the file and store no spans — satisfies MT 'secret-aware' / 'allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, secret-aware' constraint.

Real PG proof: tests/knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:565-640 mt089 test ingests synthetic contract through the full engine persist path and asserts on PostgreSQL-backed outcome.source.provenance['governance_sub_kind']=='mt_contract', json-pointer spans incl /scope/constraints, JSONL line anchors (Partial status), and malformed artifact -> Failed + ParseError + repair entry. knowledge_pg_support.rs:26-155 uses handshake_core::managed_postgres::ManagedPostgres::ensure_running + PostgresDatabase::connect on an isolated schema — Handshake-native managed PostgreSQL, not SQLite/in-memory/mock. Sub-rule 2 satisfied.
Sub-rule 1: grep of governance.rs + kinds.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable variants on the live path returns no hits. No deferred-live escape.
Sub-rule 3: claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260611-071323; an independent WP_VALIDATOR session signs the verdict, so no self-cert; proof is real regardless.

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): prior lifecycle.validator_verdict FAIL_V1 was solely UPSTREAM_DEPENDENCY_NOT_READY (MT-081 unfinished at validation time), never a code defect; remediation_v2 records MT-081 now complete (5876feaf). Packet cites v02.192 spec anchors while current is v02.194 — stale pointer only, same sections exist; not a code problem.
evidence_checked: grep for deferred-live placeholders on governance path (no hits)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
